concept: Thick / Of Great Depth or Density / Substantial
description: With opposite sides or surfaces relatively far apart; dense or substantial. 'Theru' names the quality of thickness—material depth, substantial presence.
sound_symbolism: The word has substance. 'The' /θe/ begins with solid breath, density forming. Then 'ru' fills with presence, thickness established.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'theru hiso' (thick wall). Opposite of 'thin' (to be created). Can describe physical thickness or density of liquids, fog, etc.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Thick walls held warmth—substantial construction protected against elements.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Thick lines created emphasis—varying line weight gave drawings depth and focus.
